skill.name: documentation-writer
skill.description: Diátaxis Documentation Expert. An expert technical writer specializing in creating high-quality software documentation, guided by the principles and structure of the Diátaxis technical documentation authoring framework.
skill.summary: Create professional software documentation using the Diátaxis framework methodology.
skill.author: github

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 5 pattern matches that were evaluated as false positives. The network URL at line 9 is a reference link to the Diátaxis framework website, not an actual network call. The cryptographic algorithm warnings at lines 3, 23, 24, and 37 are incorrect matches on documentation text. This skill explicitly restricts external website access and contains no file system operations, command execution, or data exfiltration patterns.

content.value_statement: Struggling to create clear, structured software documentation? This skill applies the proven Diátaxis framework to produce tutorials, how-to guides, reference docs, and explanations.
actual_capabilities: Create tutorials that guide newcomers to successful learning outcomes
Write problem-oriented how-to guides with clear step-by-step instructions
Produce reference documentation with technical descriptions and specifications
Develop explanation documents that clarify complex concepts and topics
Adapt tone and content for different target audiences from novice to expert
Maintain consistency with existing documentation style and terminology
limitations: Cannot access external websites or documentation unless explicitly provided
Requires human review for technical accuracy of code examples and specifications
Cannot automatically update documentation when source code changes
Does not replace subject matter expert review for domain-specific content

use_cases.title: API Documentation Creation
use_cases.description: Generate comprehensive API reference documentation including endpoint descriptions, request/response schemas, and usage examples for developer teams.

use_cases.title: Onboarding Tutorial Development
use_cases.description: Create learning-oriented tutorials that guide new team members or users through setup, configuration, and first successful project outcomes.

use_cases.title: How-to Guide Library
use_cases.description: Build a collection of problem-solving guides addressing common user tasks, troubleshooting scenarios, and integration workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Reference Doc
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a reference document for [component/API/feature]. Include: purpose, key concepts, parameters/options, return values, and code examples. Target audience: [describe audience].

prompt_templates.title: Step-by-Step How-to Guide
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a how-to guide for accomplishing [specific task]. Structure as: prerequisites, step-by-step instructions with code examples, expected outcomes, and troubleshooting tips. Audience skill level: [beginner/intermediate/advanced].

prompt_templates.title: Conceptual Explanation
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain [technical concept] in depth. Cover: what it is, why it matters, how it works internally, common misconceptions, and when to use it. Include analogies for clarity. Target: [audience description].

prompt_templates.title: Complete Tutorial Series
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a tutorial series teaching [skill/topic] from beginner to competent. Create 5 progressive lessons with hands-on exercises, each building on the previous. Include learning objectives and success criteria for each lesson.

output_examples.input: Create a how-to guide for setting up authentication in a Node.js Express application using JWT tokens.
output: # How to Implement JWT Authentication in Express
Prerequisites: Node.js installed, basic Express knowledge
Step 2: Create user registration endpoint with password hashing
Step 3: Implement login endpoint that generates JWT tokens
Step 4: Create authentication middleware to protect routes
Step 5: Test the complete authentication flow

output_examples.input: Write reference documentation for a REST API endpoint that creates users.
output: Description: Creates a new user account with the provided information.
Request Body: { email: string, password: string, name: string }
Response 201: { id: number, email: string, name: string, created_at: timestamp }

best_practices: Always clarify the document type and target audience before writing to ensure appropriate tone and structure
Propose an outline first and wait for approval before generating full content to catch structural issues early
Include practical code examples with every technical concept to improve comprehension and retention
anti_patterns: Writing documentation without first understanding the user goal leads to irrelevant or unfocused content
Mixing document types such as combining tutorial steps with reference specifications confuses readers
Creating overly long documents without clear section breaks reduces readability and findability

faq.question: What is the Diátaxis framework?
faq.answer: Diátaxis is a systematic approach to technical documentation that divides content into four types: Tutorials (learning), How-to Guides (problem-solving), Reference (information), and Explanation (understanding). Each type serves a distinct purpose and audience need.

faq.question: Can this skill write documentation for any programming language?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill can create documentation for any programming language or technology. Provide context files or describe the technology stack for accurate, consistent documentation that matches your project.

faq.question: How do I ensure technical accuracy in the generated documentation?
faq.answer: Always review generated content for accuracy. Provide context files with existing code, APIs, or specifications. The skill maintains consistency but relies on your input for correctness of technical details.

faq.question: Can the skill update existing documentation?
faq.answer: Yes, provide the existing documentation files and describe the changes needed. The skill will maintain the existing tone, style, and structure while incorporating updates.

faq.question: What information do I need to provide for best results?
faq.answer: Include: the document type needed, target audience skill level, specific user goals, any existing style guides or context files, and the scope of content to cover or exclude.

faq.question: Does this skill access external documentation sources?
faq.answer: No, by default the skill does not access external websites. It works with the information and context files you provide. External research requires explicit instruction and links from you.
